welcome: Hi! I'm Rex the excavation robot! I just arrived at the dig site, but I don't know anything about dinosaurs yet. Can you teach me?
menu_subtitle: Collect all your dig tools and discover dinosaurs!
menu_mission_complete: Amazing! You built a complete dinosaur skeleton!
q1_start: First, let's learn about dinosaur types! Every dinosaur belongs to a group. Help me figure out which one!
q1_instruction: Is this dinosaur a carnivore, herbivore, flying reptile, marine reptile, or armored dino? Tap to label each one!
q1_done: Awesome! You taught me about dinosaur types! Let's see how well I learned.
q1_learned: You learned that AI needs labeled examples to classify things, just like sorting fossils into groups!
t_carni_trex_y: Yes! T-Rex was a fierce carnivore with tiny arms and huge teeth!
t_carni_trex_n: Actually, T-Rex was a carnivore! Look at those giant teeth!
t_carni_veloci_y: Right! Velociraptor was a fast, clever carnivore!
t_carni_veloci_n: Oops! Velociraptor was a carnivore, a speedy hunter!
t_herbi_trice_y: Correct! Triceratops was a herbivore with three horns!
t_herbi_trice_n: Actually, Triceratops was a herbivore! It ate plants, not meat!
t_herbi_brachio_y: Yes! Brachiosaurus was a gentle giant herbivore!
t_herbi_brachio_n: Oops! Brachiosaurus was a herbivore with a super long neck for reaching treetops!
t_flying_ptera_y: Right! Pteranodon was a flying reptile that soared through the sky!
t_flying_ptera_n: Actually, Pteranodon was a flying reptile! It had huge wings!
t_flying_quetz_y: Correct! Quetzalcoatlus was the biggest flying reptile ever!
t_flying_quetz_n: Oops! Quetzalcoatlus was a flying reptile, as tall as a giraffe!
t_marine_mosa_y: Yes! Mosasaurus was a marine reptile that ruled the ancient seas!
t_marine_mosa_n: Actually, Mosasaurus was a marine reptile! It lived in the ocean!
t_marine_plesio_y: Right! Plesiosaur was a marine reptile with a very long neck!
t_marine_plesio_n: Oops! Plesiosaur was a marine reptile that swam in ancient oceans!
t_armored_ankylo_y: Correct! Ankylosaurus was an armored dino with a club tail!
t_armored_ankylo_n: Actually, Ankylosaurus was an armored dino! It was like a living tank!
t_armored_stego_y: Yes! Stegosaurus was an armored dino with plates on its back!
t_armored_stego_n: Oops! Stegosaurus was an armored dino with cool back plates!
correct: Great job! You got it right!
summary: Let's look at what I learned from your fossil labels!
summary_bias: Hmm, most of your data is about one type. I might not recognize the others very well!
summary_learned: You learned that AI can be biased if it doesn't get enough examples of everything!
q3_start: Now let's test me! I'll try to classify fossils using what you taught me. Watch carefully!
q3_done: I did pretty well! Your training data really helped!
q3_retrain: I need more training. Let's go back and teach me more!
q3_learned: You learned that testing AI is important. If it makes mistakes, we need more data!
q4_start: Time to excavate! I'll scan the dig site for fossils, but sometimes I see things that are just rocks. You decide what to dig up!
q4_save: Great find! That's a real fossil!
q4_crash: Oh no! We missed that fossil!
q4_false_alarm: That was just a rock! I got confused!
q4_done: Dig complete! We found some amazing fossils together!
q4_learned: You learned that humans and AI work best as a team! AI scans fast, but humans make the final call!
q5_start: Final mission! Let's assemble the dinosaur skeleton from all the fossils we found!
q5_step1: Sorting the fossils! Laying out all the bones we found!
q5_step2: Building the skull! Connecting the jaw and teeth!
q5_step3: Attaching the spine! The vertebrae are clicking into place!
q5_step4: Adding the ribs! The rib cage is taking shape!
q5_step5: Mounting the legs! The dinosaur is standing tall!
q5_step6: Skeleton complete! What a magnificent dinosaur!
q5_done: You did it! The skeleton is complete! What a discovery!
q5_learned: You learned that AI helps scientists reconstruct the past from data!
all_done: Congratulations! You completed the Dino Digger Quest! You're a real paleontologist!
